req.title: Submit timesheet — variant 3
req.statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a weekly timesheet submission with two orthogonal constraints: per-day hours in [0,24] and weekly total in [0,80]. A webhook must fire on success. Test design uses EP to confirm the happy path, BVA to probe the exact boundaries (0, 24, 80), NEG for out-of-range per-day and over-limit weekly totals, STC to verify the draft->submitted state transition, and DT to cover the combined constraint table. Placeholders replace full auth/client helpers to stay within the 40-line cap.

tests.name: test_submit_valid_timesheet_emits_webhook
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload with 5 working days each having 8 hours (total=40)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
Poll webhook listener for the emitted event
expected: Webhook listener receives exactly one event with timesheet_id matching response
trace: R-WEB-069: valid per-day hours (8) in partition [0,24]
R-WEB-069: valid weekly total (40) in partition [0,80]
R-WEB-069: webhook event emitted for external consumers
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_submit_valid_timesheet_emits_webhook(auth_client, webhook_listener):
    payload = {
        "week": <current_week_fixture>,
        "days": {
            "mon": 8, "tue": 8, "wed": 8, "thu": 8, "fri": 8,
            "sat": 0, "sun": 0
        }
    }
    resp = auth_client.post("/timesheets/submit", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "submitted"
    ts_id = body["timesheet_id"]
    event = webhook_listener.wait_for_event(timeout=5)
    assert event is not None
    assert event["timesheet_id"] == ts_id
    assert event["event_type"] == "timesheet.submitted"

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_per_day_hours_exceed_24_returns_422
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload with one day having 25 hours (outside [0,24])
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
expected: Response body contains error referencing per-day hour constraint
trace: R-WEB-069: error pathway — 422 when per-day hours outside 0-24
R-WEB-069: value 25 is in the invalid equivalence partition (>24)

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_boundary_max_daily_and_max_weekly
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload with mon-fri each having 16 hours and sat 0, sun 0 (total=80, max valid weekly)
Ensure each day value equals exactly 24 for a separate boundary probe on daily max
POST the 80-hour weekly total payload to /timesheets/submit
expected: HTTP 200 response for total=80 (upper boundary, inclusive)
trace: R-WEB-069: BVA upper boundary weekly total = 80 (max valid)

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_boundary_min_zero_hours
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload with all days having 0 hours (total=0, lower boundary)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
expected: HTTP 200 response (0 is valid lower boundary)
trace: R-WEB-069: BVA lower boundary per-day = 0 and weekly total = 0 (min valid)
R-WEB-069: both constraints satisfied at minimum

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_weekly_total_exceeds_80_returns_422
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload where weekly total equals 81 (one above the maximum)
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
expected: Response body references weekly total constraint violation
trace: R-WEB-069: error pathway — 422 when weekly total outside 0-80
R-WEB-069: BVA value 81 is first invalid value above upper boundary

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_state_transitions_draft_to_submitted
preconditions: A timesheet exists with state=draft for the current week
actions: Verify initial timesheet state is draft via GET /timesheets/current
Re-fetch timesheet and verify state changed to submitted
expected: State transition from draft to submitted is recorded
trace: R-WEB-069: STC — timesheet must transition from draft to submitted
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_submit_timesheet_state_transitions_draft_to_submitted(auth_client):
    pre = auth_client.get("/timesheets/current")
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json()["state"] == "draft"
    payload = {
        "week": pre.json()["week"],
        "days": {
            "mon": 8, "tue": 8, "wed": 8, "thu": 8, "fri": 8,
            "sat": 0, "sun": 0
        }
    }
    resp = auth_client.post("/timesheets/submit", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    post = auth_client.get("/timesheets/current")
    assert post.status_code == 200
    assert post.json()["state"] == "submitted"

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_decision_table_both_constraints_violated
preconditions: A draft timesheet exists for the current week
actions: Build payload where one day exceeds 24 hours AND weekly total exceeds 80
POST to /timesheets/submit with valid auth token
expected: Response body lists both per-day and weekly total violations
trace: R-WEB-069: DT — condition combination: per-day>24 AND weekly>80 -> 422
R-WEB-069: all invalid constraint combinations must be rejected
